title: Getting Started with Tailwind CSS
content:
### Installation via npm The recommended way to install Tailwind CSS is through npm: ```bash npm install -D tailwindcss ``` ### Initialize Configuration Generate a `tailwind.config.js` file: ```bash npx tailwindcss init ``` This creates a minimal config file: ```js /** @type {import('tailwindcss').Config} */ module.exports = { content: [ "./src/**/*.{html,js}", ], theme: { extend: {}, }, plugins: [], } ``` ### Content Paths The `content` array tells Tailwind which files to scan for class names: ```js module.exports = { content: [ "./pages/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}", "./components/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}", "./index.html", ], } ``` ### @tailwind Directives Add Tailwind's layers to your main CSS file: ```css @tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities; ``` - **base** - Resets and default styles (like normalize.css) - **components** - Component classes (used with @apply) - **utilities** - All utility classes ### CDN Usage For quick prototyping, use the Play CDN: ```html

Hello World

``` ### PostCSS Setup Add Tailwind as a PostCSS plugin in `postcss.config.js`: ```js module.exports = { plugins: { tailwindcss: {}, autoprefixer: {}, }, } ``` Then run your build command: ```bash npx tailwindcss -i ./src/input.css -o ./dist/output.css --watch ```
codeExample:
Welcome

Tailwind is set up and ready!

options: Which files to scan for class names
